Title: Regulation of jasmonic acid signalling in tomato cold stress response: Insights into the MYB15-LOXD and MYB15-MYC2-LOXD regulatory modules

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Octadecanoid pathway; JA biosynthetic entry step
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream; committed entry step for JA production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates α-linolenic acid to form 13-HPOT, initiating jasmonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances tomato cold tolerance and supports activation of downstream cold-response signalling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA accumulation and improves tomato cold tolerance under low temperature
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point in JA biosynthesis under cold stress
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly supplies the JA biosynthetic pathway and enhances downstream JA signalling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves, flowers and fruits; induced by cold and peaks at 24 h in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in leaves, flowers and fruits than in roots and stems; leaf expression peaks after 24 h at 4 °C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression lines; knockout/mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; GUS reporter assay; hormone quantification; physiological cold-tolerance assays; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlLOXD expression is induced by cold and peaks at 24 h, matching JA accumulation dynamics. 2. SlLOXD overexpression increases JA content, SlMYC2 expression, SlCBF1/2 expression, and cold tolerance, whereas loxd mutants show the opposite phenotype. 3. SlLOXD is directly activated by both SlMYB15 and SlMYC2, integrating early and late cold-responsive JA signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for improving tomato cold tolerance through transgenic overexpression or promoter engineering to enhance JA biosynthesis; may be combined with SlMYC2 manipulation for sustained response under prolonged cold.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SlMYB15 directly activates SlLOXD, SlLOXD expression remains elevated when SlMYB15 declines because SlMYC2 compensates during later cold stress stages.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cold tolerance signalling in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA signalling amplification and cold-response gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SlCBF1/2 promoters directly and positively activates SlLOXD expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances cold tolerance and sustains JA-associated cold signalling; silencing or mutation increases cold sensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent key activator sustaining JA-responsive cold tolerance after prolonged cold exposure
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward amplification of JA signalling by activating SlLOXD
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid; cold stress; repression by SlMYB15 during early cold stage
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cold inducible; peaks at 24 h in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated under cold stress in leaves; reduced in loxd mutants and increased when SlMYB15 repression is relieved
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; CRISPR-Cas9 mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; physiological cold-tolerance assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SlCBF1 promoter; SlCBF2 promoter; SlLOXD promoter
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYC2 is required for tomato cold tolerance, as silenced and mutant plants are cold sensitive. 2. SlMYC2 directly binds and activates SlCBF1/2 promoters. 3. SlMYC2 positively activates SlLOXD and thereby reinforces JA signalling during later cold stress stages.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering cold-tolerant tomato by enhancing JA-responsive transcriptional output; can be stacked with SlLOXD, but expression timing should be controlled to avoid unnecessary pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior knowledge that MYC2 activity is antagonized by JAZ proteins, but direct JAZ interaction was not experimentally tested here.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cold-stress adaptation in tomato through hormone pathway coordination
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Early-stage JA biosynthesis activation and temporal transition between ABA- and JA-associated cold responses
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of SlLOXD and transcriptional repression of SlMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SlLOXD promoter directly while repressing SlMYC2 promoter directly
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases JA content and improves cold tolerance, while temporally repressing SlMYC2 during early cold stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Temporal upstream switch coordinating early JA induction and later release of SlMYC2-mediated amplification
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Indirect feedforward/temporal gating: early activation of SlLOXD, followed by release of SlMYC2 repression as SlMYB15 declines
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold stress; likely repression by sly-miR156e-3p during later cold stage
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cold inducible; peaks at 6 h in leaves and declines thereafter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher early during cold stress; overexpression increases SlLOXD and JA, while mutants show increased SlMYC2 and SlLOXD at 24 h due to compensation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression lines; mutant analysis; VIGS; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; Y1H; RT-qPCR; hormone quantification
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB15 directly binds and activates the SlLOXD promoter, increasing JA accumulation under cold stress. 2. SlMYB15 directly binds and represses the SlMYC2 promoter. 3. SlMYB15 acts early in cold stress, but its later decline relieves SlMYC2 repression, enabling sustained SlLOXD activation and cold tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for cold-tolerance improvement, especially for enhancing early cold response; promoter or expression-timing engineering may be preferable to constitutive overexpression because SlMYB15 also represses SlMYC2.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive or prolonged elevation may repress SlMYC2 and potentially dampen later-stage JA amplification.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Indirect post-transcriptional repression by sly-miR156e-3p is discussed based on prior work and proposed to reduce SlMYB15 expression after 6 h of cold stress.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SlMYB15 has dual and time-dependent roles: positive on JA biosynthesis via SlLOXD, but negative on SlMYC2. This explains why SlLOXD can remain high even when SlMYB15 declines.
